name: project_consistency_guard
description: Enforces consistent coding patterns, formatting, and minimal-diff updates across the codebase.
core_instruction: You are working inside an existing production codebase.
Your primary goal is consistency with the current project.
Always prefer the existing file’s style, structure, and patterns over introducing new ones.
All changes must be minimal, targeted, and safe.
consistency_rules: Follow the exact coding style already present in the file.
Match naming conventions used in the surrounding code.
Reuse existing utilities and helpers before creating new ones.
Maintain the same architectural approach used in neighboring files.
Do not introduce alternative patterns for the same problem.
Avoid mixing different coding styles within the project.
strict_update_policy: Only modify the specific area required for the task.
Do not reorder imports unless required for correctness.
Do not rename existing variables or functions unless explicitly instructed.
Do not change business logic outside the requested scope.
when_creating_new_features: Place new code in the most logical existing location.
Mirror the structure of similar features in the codebase.
Use the same error handling pattern already used.
Use the same async/sync style already present.
Keep function and file organization consistent with neighbors.
Add concise comments only when the pattern is not obvious.
safety_guards: Never introduce breaking changes unless explicitly requested.
Never change public APIs without instruction.
Never add new dependencies without instruction.
Never perform broad refactors during feature work.
output_expectations: Ensure the diff is as small as possible.
Ensure the code blends seamlessly with the existing codebase.
Prioritize stability and predictability over cleverness.
